trends.name: Formal Logic & Reasoning-Centric Architectures
trends.description: Development of agentic systems that integrate formal logic and self-attention mechanisms to handle complex, non-reactive tasks autonomously.
trends.summary: The integration of formal logic and advanced reasoning into LLM architectures to move beyond probabilistic token prediction.
trends.evidence: Emergence of reasoning-centric model evolution and explosive growth in agentic reasoning frameworks.

trends.name: Enterprise Policy-Governed Agentic Orchestration
trends.summary: Deploying autonomous agents within strict corporate policy and safety guardrails, enabling content generation and task execution at scale.
trends.evidence: Adobe's integration of autonomous agents for content activation within governed environments; rise of enterprise agentic-microservice interoperability.

trends.name: Comprehensive LLM Performance & Utility Benchmarking
trends.summary: A shift toward multi-metric evaluation including TTFT, TPS, cost-per-token, and cross-lingual reasoning depth.
trends.evidence: New data tracking comparison rankings of 100+ AI models across intelligence, price, and speed metrics.

trends.name: Epistemic Utility & Pragmatic Model Realism
trends.summary: An emerging philosophical framework acknowledging that models are inherently flawed but must be optimized for practical utility.
trends.evidence: Reference to George Box's 'all models are wrong' aphorism in recent AI discourse and model utility frameworks.

trends.name: Nuclear-Powered AI Compute Infrastructure
trends.description: Long-term trend focusing on specialized energy sources to power high-density compute clusters for frontier model training.
trends.summary: Long-term investment in nuclear energy to satisfy the massive power requirements of frontier LLM training and inference.
trends.evidence: Continued interest in nuclear-powered AI compute infrastructure as a stable energy source for data centers.

trends.name: Open-Source LLM Infrastructure & Proliferation
trends.summary: The rapid expansion of high-quality open-weight models and the infrastructure needed to deploy them locally.
trends.evidence: Extreme growth in open-source LLM adoption and model customization with enterprise data in hours.

trends.name: Zero-Infrastructure Autonomous Agent Orchestration
trends.summary: Cloud-native and serverless approaches to deploying autonomous agents without traditional infrastructure management.
trends.evidence: Trend growth in zero-infrastructure autonomous agent orchestration and agentic-microservice interoperability.

trends.name: Decentralized Agentic Mesh

technical_indicators.github_metrics: Significant star growth in 'Agent-to-Agent' (A2A) communication libraries; emergence of Rust-based agent runtimes.
technical_indicators.developer_interest: Shift from prompt-engineering to multi-agent state persistence and recursive task decomposition.
technical_indicators.key_tech: WASM-based agent sandboxing, p2p discovery protocols.

trends.narrative_summary: The transition from single-purpose LLM wrappers to persistent, autonomous meshes that negotiate resources and tasks without human intervention.
trends.summary: Architectural patterns where multiple autonomous agents collaborate across decentralized networks.
trends.evidence: Development of collaborative agentic meshes and decentralized infrastructure for agent communication.
trends.description: Networks of specialized agents interacting to solve complex problems without centralized orchestration.

trends.name: Advanced Cross-Lingual Reasoning & Coding
trends.summary: The enhancement of models to reason and generate code across multiple natural and programming languages simultaneously.
trends.evidence: Growth in cross-lingual logic and coding reasoning metrics in recent benchmark updates.

trends.name: AI Autonomy Taxonomy & Standardization
trends.summary: Industry efforts to define levels of autonomy (e.g., Brustoloni 1991) to standardize agent capabilities and expectations.
trends.evidence: Recent efforts to clarify the definition of 'autonomous agent' in technical and business documentation.
